items.title: AI at Meta: Meta AI Products, Models and Research
items.author: ai.meta.com
items.snippet: Meta's AI blog in 2026 highlights several major open source model releases. Most notably, Meta introduced the Llama 4 herd — including Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick — marking the beginning of a new era of natively multimodal AI. These are the first open-weight models built with a mixture-of-experts (MoE) architecture and feature unprecedented context window support. Both models are available on llama.com and Hugging Face, as well as across major cloud platforms and edge silicon providers.

items.title: Introducing Microsoft 365 Copilot – your copilot for work - The Official Microsoft Blog
items.author: blogs.microsoft.com
items.snippet: Microsoft's Copilot ecosystem has seen significant evolution in early 2026, with the company pivoting from assistive AI toward fully agentic capabilities. Key announcements include Wave 3 of Microsoft 365 Copilot, which introduces expanded model diversity (including Claude and next-generation OpenAI models), and the general availability of Agent 365 on May 1 for $15/user/month. Agent 365 serves as an enterprise control plane to observe, govern, and secure all AI agents running across an organiza

items.title: Spain's Xoople raises $130 million Series B to map the Earth for AI
items.author: techcrunch.com
items.snippet: AI startup funding activity in early 2026 has been historic, shattering previous records. Q1 2026 saw startup funding reach all-time highs, driven largely by massive AI rounds. OpenAI raised $110 billion in one of the largest private funding rounds in history, pushing its valuation past $852 billion. xAI (Elon Musk's AI company) closed a $20 billion Series E, while Humans& — a 'human-centric' AI startup founded by alumni from Anthropic, xAI, and Google — raised a $480 million seed round. items.title: GitHub Trending Weekly 2026-04-08: Skills Ecosystem Explosion
items.author: www.shareuhack.com
items.snippet: As of mid-April 2026, the GitHub AI agent ecosystem is experiencing explosive growth, with several standout repositories dominating weekly trending charts. The most viral breakout is OpenClaw, which surged from ~9,000 to over 210,000 stars in roughly 60 days — one of the fastest-growing open-source projects in GitHub history. NousResearch's Hermes Agent (self-improving AI agent framework, 35k+ stars) has held a top-5 trending spot for multiple consecutive weeks, with +10,487 stars gained in a si

items.title: Release v4.51.0: Llama 4, Phi4-Multimodal, DeepSeek-v3, Qwen3 · huggingface/transformers
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: The open-source LLM landscape in early 2026 has seen major new releases and active benchmarking. Hugging Face Transformers v4.51.0 added support for Llama 4, Phi4-Multimodal, DeepSeek-V3, and Qwen3 architectures, with Llama 4 and Qwen3 representing the latest generation of open-weight frontier models. Google's Gemma 4 also launched with strong multimodal performance across reasoning, coding, vision, and long-context tasks. The Open LLM Leaderboard on Hugging Face continues to be the canonical be

items.title: GitHub - alvinreal/awesome-opensource-ai: Curated list of the best truly open-source AI projects, models, tools, and infrastructure.
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: April 2026 has seen a wave of significant open-source AI model releases, with major tech companies pushing competitive alternatives to proprietary models. Google released Gemma 4 in four sizes (E2B, E4B, 26B MoE, 31B Dense) under the Apache 2.0 license, focusing on agentic workflows and complex reasoning. items.title: Large Language Models Miss the Multi-Agent Mark
items.author: arxiv.org
items.snippet: This week's arxiv activity on AI agents and LLMs shows strong momentum across three themes. First, comprehensive surveys are mapping the agent landscape: 'Large Language Model Agent: A Survey on Methodology, Applications and Challenges' (2503.21460) and 'From LLM Reasoning to Autonomous AI Agents: A Comprehensive Review' (2504.19678) both synthesize ~60 benchmarks and propose taxonomies covering reasoning, code generation, and autonomous decision-making. items.title: Cody - Sourcegraph docs
items.author: sourcegraph.com
items.snippet: Cursor has been one of the most active AI coding assistants in early 2026, shipping several major updates. In March, they released Composer 2 with frontier-level coding performance on challenging tasks, followed by Cursor 3 which introduces a new parallel agent interface allowing multiple agents to run simultaneously across repos, locally, in worktrees, in the cloud, and on remote SSH. In April, they shipped Bugbot improvements including self-improvement capabilities in real time, MCP support, a
